title: selaginella pulvinata
intro: Selaginella pulvinata, also known as the resin fern, is a species of fern-like plant that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Selaginella pulvinata include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in the form of teas, infusions, or decoctions, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
